source: Original — Mörk Borg Module Runner settlement generator. Flavored after canonical regions, powers and cults; wording original.

regions.name: Tveland
regions.flavor: the untamed wilds that gnaw at the road
goods: a Galgenbeck deathmask
a vial of goblin ichor
a two-headed silver ring
a wickhead's pickled brain
a stoppered jar marked GRAVEN-TOSK SOIL
a basilisk pendant, both heads still attached
a coil of black hair from a hermit who didn't have hair
a leather scroll case carved with two facing snakes
a sealed letter addressed to Josilfa Migol
a forked tongue, pickled, still moist

regions.name: the Western Kingdom
regions.flavor: the dry, dead Wästland under a paranoid king
goods: a pouch of valley vapours
a jar of (genuine) troll piss
a pilgrim's compass that points at your death
a lucky fishing spear, luck expired
a fragment of mad King Fathmu's portrait, torn in anger
a dried fish that wasn't a fish when caught
a Schleswig brothel-token still warm
a tin of marsh-salve that smells of someone else's grief
a peasant's kuksa carved with three faces
a relic ring missing the relic

regions.name: Kergüs
regions.flavor: the frozen plains where the cold is patient
goods: a vibrant red ribbon
a vial of glowing blue blood
a gleaming golden scalpel
a one-eyed one-legged one-winged gull
a Blood-Countess token, unauthorised
a length of black-glass from an Alliáns spire
a vial of melted ice that does not freeze again
a bone-knife with the maker's tooth still in the haft
a frozen flower in a glass case
a piece of fur that is colder than the air around it

regions.name: Sarkash
regions.flavor: the forest that spreads where no one is watching
goods: a fistful of grave-soil that won't dry
a lantern that draws the fog
a dog-whistle only the dead obey
a goblin's tongue, salt-cured
a length of root that pulses, faintly
a hunter's mark carved on bone, still wet
a small bell that summons only itself
a folded map of paths that don't exist anymore
a flask of resin from a tree that no longer stands
a child's drawing of the howler bear, drawn from life

regions.name: the Grift coast
regions.flavor: the bridge-city's edge above the bottomless Múr
goods: a key retrieved from the Múr
a sorrowful music box
a hangman's rope, lightly used
a calendar of despair, one page left
a sea-shell that still hears the screaming
a Sigfúm-blessed coin, edge worn smooth
a bottle of seaweed wine from before the apocalypse-talk began
a bridge-toll receipt with no date
a fisherman's hook bent in the shape of a question
a glass float, empty of the thing it once held

powers.ruler: a tax-farmer loyal to mad King Fathmu IX
a Basilisk inquisitor who corrects heretics in public and at length
a 'kind' death-priest preparing everyone for the cliff
a blood-noble who has not aged a day in thirty years
a merchant guild that owns the only clean water
no one — a mob with a rope and a reason
a hidden thing in the cellar that the elders feed and obey
a guildmaster who has bought every house in the district
a brother-and-sister pair who agree on nothing except the executions
a foreign envoy whose embassy has become a prison
a war-veteran with no war left to fight
a midwife who delivers everyone, the living and the otherwise
a ferryman who has not crossed the river in years
a child-king regent until they reach an age they will never reach
a man with two shadows and no answer for it
a circle of seven elders who no longer remember each other's names

factions: the Two-Headed Basilisks and their Inquisition
the Cult of the Dark Spider
a guild of grave-robbers who call themselves undertakers
a band of road-priests who collect tolls in confession
the white-eyed children of the marsh-house, who are not exactly children
a wandering order of the Bone Pilgrimage
a brotherhood of plague-doctors who are not all doctors
the sleeping-cult, who do not believe in the waking world
a debtors' assembly that has begun to take debts in flesh
a chapter of the Sons of the Cold Bell
the surviving merchants of an extinct guild, still trading in its name

location_types.title: the tavern
location_types.atmosphere: Smoke, sour ale, and the wet cough of the dying. Best informed room in town.

sub.title: The Smoke-Black Backroom
sub.atmosphere: Where the deals nobody mentions are made, behind a door that locks both ways.

location_types.title: The Plague-Pit Market
location_types.atmosphere: Stalls of dubious meat ring a pit that nobody admits to digging.

sub.title: Beneath the Stalls
sub.atmosphere: A trapdoor leads to a cold pit of unsold things and unsold people.

location_types.title: Shrine of the Two-Headed Basilisks
location_types.atmosphere: Two stone heads, one always watching. Tallow, ash, and dried blood.

sub.title: The Reliquary Crypt
sub.atmosphere: Bone-saints stacked like cordwood. The youngest is still moist.

location_types.title: The Gallows Square
location_types.atmosphere: Three ropes — two occupied, one swinging empty and patient.

sub.title: Under the Drop
sub.atmosphere: Where the cut-down dead are bagged for the pit. Some bags are warm.

location_types.title: The Apothecary's Shoppe
location_types.atmosphere: Jars of cures that cure nothing, labelled in a hopeful hand.

sub.title: The Apothecary's Cellar
sub.atmosphere: Jars of things that should not have been jarred. The labels are in a hand that is not the apothecary's.

location_types.title: The Weeping Well
location_types.atmosphere: Brown water that tastes of someone else's grief.

sub.title: The Diver's Ledge
sub.atmosphere: A narrow ledge inside the well-shaft. Someone goes down on a rope; someone else hopes they come back.

location_types.title: The Bone-Orchard
location_types.atmosphere: Fresh mounds outnumber the old. The soil will not settle.

sub.title: The Caretaker's Hut
sub.atmosphere: Tools, ledgers of the dead, and one fresh bed nobody has slept in.

location_types.title: The Rotting Docks
location_types.atmosphere: Black water, dead nets, a boat that keeps returning empty-crewed.

sub.title: Under the Pilings
sub.atmosphere: Brackish dark where things wash up the wrong way and stay.

location_types.title: The Midnight Market
location_types.atmosphere: Lanterns shuttered. Everything is for sale here, including you.

sub.title: The Cellar Behind the Cellar
sub.atmosphere: Past two false walls. The price for being here is being seen.

location_types.title: The Bridge-Toll Station
location_types.atmosphere: A creaking span and a collector who has never been late.

sub.title: Under the Span
sub.atmosphere: Where the bodies the toll-collector keeps end up.

location_types.title: The Sleeping-House
location_types.atmosphere: Rows of cots. None of the sleepers wake at noon.

sub.title: The Dreamer's Cellar
sub.atmosphere: The cot they never let anyone see.

location_types.title: The Coal-Yard
location_types.atmosphere: Black piles, black hands, black water at the edges.

sub.title: Behind the Pile
sub.atmosphere: A heap that warms itself, and what built the heap.

location_types.title: The Surgery
location_types.atmosphere: Cleaner than the rest of town. Smells of vinegar and worse.

sub.title: The Sample Room
sub.atmosphere: Jars in straight rows. Some labeled, some not.

location_types.title: The Salt-House
location_types.atmosphere: Floor inches deep in brine-crust. Things kept here keep too well.

sub.title: The Brine Vault
sub.atmosphere: A cold room kept colder. Some of the salt is moving.

location_types.title: The Bell-Yard
location_types.atmosphere: Bells in every size, swung by hands you can't see. The smallest one is the loudest.

sub.title: The Founder's Pit
sub.atmosphere: Where the bells were cast. The metal is still warm.

problems: Conscripts and travellers vanish on the west road, and the count rises each new moon.
A plague with no name walks door to door, polite as a tax-man.
The well has named a child it wants, and the season turns soon.
A Basilisk's demand has come due and no one here can pay it.
The dead will not stay buried, and they remember their debts.
A heretic-hunt is on; anyone may be denounced for a coin.
The grain is gone, and what is being sold as meat is not.
A relic was stolen from the shrine and the saints have stopped pretending to listen.
Every dog in town has stopped barking. Nobody dare say why.
The river runs the wrong way during the night, and brings things back.
Children have started repeating a hymn no adult here taught them.
The miller's wheel turns without water, and grinds something other than grain.
The grain-store burned last week. The flame, by all accounts, was blue.
The constable is missing. So are his keys. So are several locks.
The dead in the bone-yard have been turning to face north, as a body.
A stranger arrived in a coach without horses. The coach is still there.
A bell that hasn't rung in a century rang at midnight. Twice.
Three babies born last month all have the same birthmark, in the same place.
The vinegar in every barrel turned to brine overnight. The brine is warm.
The hunters are coming back without their dogs, with stories they will not tell.
A new shrine appeared in the square. Nobody built it. It is full of offerings.
The town's water-witch has cut her hair, packed her things, and refuses to say goodbye.
The shadows here fall in the wrong direction at noon.
The town's chronicle has begun writing tomorrow's entries today.
rumors: "The priest's shadow falls the wrong way. Watch him at noon."
"Whatever you do, don't drink from the well after dark."
"The tax-man hasn't been seen in a week. Nor has his skin."
"There's a door in the cellar that wasn't there last winter."
"The new graves dig themselves open again by morning."
"Pay the smiling ones no mind. They're counting us."
"A stranger paid in old coin — the kind they bury you with."
"The bell rings itself when someone in town is about to die."
"Don't look at the inquisitor's left hand. He keeps it gloved for a reason."
"The miller's wife was buried twice. They had to."
"There's a hole in the cellar of the apothecary's. Don't ask what's in it."
"The northeast road is shorter than it should be. So is anyone who walks it."
"The grave-digger drinks alone, but never quite alone."
"A baker swears the dough rises on a different schedule now."
"Every full moon a coin appears on the gravedigger's pillow. He doesn't keep them."
"The mayor's wife has been seen at two windows at once."
"If you sleep in the hayloft you will dream of a man you've never met. You'll know him by sight forever after."
"Lock your door at the third bell. The fourth, if you can manage it."
"The blacksmith's apprentice has stopped sweating in the forge."
"The chickens have stopped laying. The eggs they laid before are still warm."
"Old Mathilde knows everyone's true name and won't say so. She'll write it down for silver."
"There's a man who only comes to market in winter. He doesn't bring his own goods."
"The graveyard is one mound bigger than it was yesterday and nobody dug it."
"A child here was born without a shadow. Nobody mentions it."
"The well-water tastes faintly of iron now. It didn't last month."
"The cooper's son went into the woods after a stray pig. Only the pig came back."
"Every dog in town turned away from the priest at last week's blessing. Every dog."
"The candle-maker has started counting under her breath, all day, every day."
"The hangman has begun to look forward to it."
"The bell-ringer says the bells ring themselves now, and he just dresses for it."
wares: a funeral coat, fine but gaudy
a deathmask of someone you almost recognise
salted meat (do not ask)
a clay kuksa carved with prayers nobody says anymore
a tooth-rattle to keep the dead asleep
a flask of something that burns going down and coming up
a candle made from a saint's tallow
a bone awl, used for piercing the soft parts of a confession
a string of teeth too small for any adult mouth
a knife that hums when meat is near
a folded prayer-flag of unknown faith
a clay cup that fills itself once per day with the wrong liquid
a length of rope that has been to a hanging
a brick from a wall that was struck by lightning
a small mirror that shows the room one day prior
a vial of grave-soil sworn to bring sleep
a brass coin minted with a face nobody remembers
a leather pouch holding three bird-skulls
a glass bead containing a single fly, perfectly preserved
a pewter spoon stamped with a king's mark from before any king
an iron nail pulled from a coffin that was buried, opened, and reburied
a parchment scrap with one true name on it
a bottle of black water from a well that has been filled in
a folded letter sealed with hair instead of wax
a small jar of frost from somewhere it cannot have come from
a child's shoe found on a road no child has ever travelled
a tooth marked with a sigil that won't wash off
a ribbon dyed in a colour that is now illegal
a wax tablet etched with a curse you cannot read
a piece of stained glass from a window that no longer exists
a censer that still smokes faintly, with nothing in it
a wooden saint with one eye broken out
a pickled finger labelled REMORSE
a comb of yellow bone, threaded with white hair
dangers: the thing the town feeds so it will keep sleeping
the man who came in last spring and never left
the hunger in the children's eyes
the new priest, who arrived with no past
the dog that doesn't bark anymore
whatever wears the dead like a coat
the silence at the bottom of the wells
the wind from the northeast, which is always wrong
the way the bells ring without hands
the lord's collectors, who collect things other than coin
the next plague, which is already on the road
the way the candles bend toward you
the small white things that come out of the ground after rain
atmospheres: A grey settlement squats under a sky like wet ash.
Smoke, frost, and the smell of a place that has stopped hoping.
Mud, bells, and shuttered windows — eyes behind every one.
The road simply ends here, the way a sentence ends when the speaker dies.
A handful of crooked roofs, all sagging the same direction.
The bells toll at no proper hour. Nobody comments.
The streets are too narrow and the smell never quite leaves them.
Three crows on every gable, watching the same nothing.
The mud here is the colour of dried blood. The blood here is the colour of mud.
Lanterns flicker in windows that should be empty.
The houses lean inward, as though listening.
A grey town like a row of bad teeth.
The graveyard is bigger than the village. It is also more crowded.
Smoke rises from chimneys whose hearths are cold.
Even the dogs are quiet here, and they are watching too.
A place where the seasons forgot to leave.
tokens: a single warm coin minted last winter
a wax-sealed envelope unopened for seventy years
the night-watchman's tally-stick with a fresh notch
a small lead figurine of the ruler made by an unknown hand
a single black feather marked with the seat's sigil
a copper key with no lock anyone admits to owning
a fragment of mirror from the chapel reliquary
a clay tablet inscribed with the seat's true name
a strip of hair tied with a faded ribbon
a tarnished medal of an order no longer in existence
the courtier's lost glove, found in a place it could not be
a folded handkerchief stiff with old, careful blood
